title: SB 362 (The DELETE ACT) Lets Californians Take Control of Their Personal Information and Require Data Brokers to Delete their Personal Information when Requested

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 362 creates a free, one-stop-shop website for Californians to control access to their personal information.",
    "Data brokers collect, analyze, and sell personal information by aggregating data from various sources.",
    "Data brokers possess thousands of data points on consumers, which include sensitive information such as reproductive healthcare and geolocation.",
    "Under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), consumers have the right to require data brokers to delete information they collected directly from them.",
    "Data brokers must register with the California Privacy Protection Agency (CPPA) and disclose the types of personal information they collect.",
    "Data brokers that fail to comply with the DELETE Act will face civil penalties and administrative fines."
  ]
}
```
